turns.user_message: I keep noticing how quick I am to read into every little pause during our conversations lately. It has been creeping up on me for months, and I just brush it off as a rough patch. Yesterday I caught myself overthinking a simple text message for way too long. I know it sounds silly, but the silence feels heavier than it used to. I just wish I could stop second-guessing every single interaction.

I have started tracking how often I hold my breath waiting for a reply, and it happens almost every day now. The waiting game has completely taken over my headspace, leaving me exhausted before the day even ends. I keep replaying old exchanges in my mind, searching for clues I probably missed the first time around. It is getting harder to separate my actual mood from the quiet moments between us. I feel like I am constantly walking on eggshells just to keep the peace.

I finally admitted to myself that I have completely lost my footing whenever things go quiet, and it feels like I am drowning in it. The constant guessing has worn me down to the point where I barely recognize my own reactions anymore. I catch myself shrinking my whole world just to avoid triggering another round of heavy silence. Every little pause now feels like a verdict, and I am running out of ways to pretend I am fine. I just want to remember what it feels like to breathe without waiting for permission.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
